title: The first page
statement:
An empty editor. Build a whole page from nothing.

Look at the target on the right.
constraints: The page declares its type, its language and its character set
At least two paragraphs, each with text inside
What it says is up to you

checks.t4: There are at least two paragraphs
checks.t9: The name is in the head

why.empty: The editor is empty. There is no starter code here — you write the page yourself, from the first line.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed. Every opening tag has a twin.
why.no-title: There is no tab name, or it is empty. That text is not shown on the page — it shows in the browser tab and in Google results.
why.no-h1: There is no big heading, or the tag is empty. An empty tag is a box with nothing in it — it counts for nothing.
why.few-p: There are not two paragraphs. The brief asks for at least two.
why.empty-p: There is a paragraph with no text in it. The check looks at all of them — one empty is enough to fail.
why.no-doctype: The document type declaration is missing. Without it the browser drops into an old mode and behaves oddly.
why.no-lang: The page does not say what language it is in, or the value is empty. A screen reader will read it with the wrong accent — literally.
why.no-charset: No character set is declared, or the value is empty. Anything outside plain English will come out as question marks or boxes.
why.title-outside: The tab name is not in the head. The body is for what a person sees. The head is for what says what this page is.
why.many-h1: There is more than one big heading. It is like the title of a book — there is one.

empty: Work top to bottom. The first line declares what kind of document this is.
Then the page opens, and inside it a head and a body. The head is for machines, the body is for people.
Head: character set and tab name. Body: heading and paragraphs.
not-closed: The number of opening and closing tags must match.
Order matters too: the body opens AFTER the head closes, not inside it.
Search with Ctrl+F for the closing sign and count.
no-doctype: It sits on the first line, before everything else.
It is not a tag — it does not close. You write it once and that is it.
no-lang: The attribute goes on the tag that opens the whole page.
The value is a short code: en for English, bg for Bulgarian. An empty value does not count.
no-charset: This line lives in the head and has no closing tag.
It is a meta line with a charset attribute. The value is utf-8.

solution.why:
The first instinct in front of an empty file is to start with what you can see — the heading, the text. And then the page starts with no head, no declared type, no language.

The browser will show it. But three invisible things will be missing: what kind of document this is, what language it is in, and what character set it uses. The first drops it into an old mode. The second misleads the screen reader. The third breaks any non-English text.
steps: First line: the document type declaration. Not a tag, does not close.
Open the page and tell it its language right away.
Head: the character set and the tab name. These are for machines, not for the eye.
Close the head, open the body.
Body: one big heading, then the paragraphs. This is where a person writes.
Close the body, close the page.
solution.code:
Rado

I am learning HTML.

This is my first page written from nothing.
solution.note: Half the skeleton is nowhere on the screen. That exact half decides how machines understand your page.
